item.title: about
item.description: brief biography 🍮 I was born in San Jose, California and grew up in Gilbert, Arizona. My education is: B.Sc. Physics & B.Sc. Astrophysics at Arizona State University (2014-2018) M.Sc. Physics at Ohio State University (2018-2021) Ph.D. Physics at Ohio State University (2018-present) I like camping, hiking, bird watching, cycling, and making pour over coffee.

item.title: contact
item.description: contact information ✉️ I can be reached via email: dnguyen.phys@gmail.com or LinkedIn messaging: https://www.linkedin.com/in/dustinnguyen24/

item.title: research
item.description: I am actively working on both machine learning and astrophysical research. In the last year, I have primarily focused on scientific machine learning. The most up-to-date list of my publications can be found on ADS or arXiv. machine learning research 🤖🧠 1 independent paper Neural ODEs/PDEs Using neural networks as individual terms within ODEs/PDEs to learn physics from data. Neural astrophysical wind models Nguyen ICML 2023 Workshop on Machine Learning for Astrophysics Application of steady-state neural astrophysical wind models to Chandra X-ray observations (submitted to NeurIPS 2023 Physical Sciences Workshop) Interpretability Reading about interpretability… no project started yet but very interested.
